evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: while maintaining high standards around data quality, scalability, and maintainability
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Mixed: 'fast-moving environment' and 'move from question to insight quickly' appear alongside repeated rigour signals like data quality, reliability, and trust in datasets, giving rough parity.
evidence.domain_risk: Building and improving the data models that support lending decisions, pricing, portfolio analysis, and investor reporting
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Fintech lender where models directly support lending decisions and investor reporting, meeting the high domain_risk override for financial reporting.
evidence.collaboration_width: analysts; product teams; backend engineers; business stakeholders
evidence.data_team_maturity: Championing standards and contributing to the improvement of our analytics engineering culture
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The analytics engineering practice and modern stack already exist and the role focuses on improving/scaling it rather than establishing it from zero, placing it at mid maturity.
evidence.jd_authorship: You'll work with a modern analytics stack centred around SQL, Snowflake, dbt, Fivetran and Claude
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools with precise application context (lending decisions, investor reporting, ELT pipelines) make the day-to-day work clear.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: You'll work closely with analysts, product teams, backend engineers, and business stakeholders
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Responsibilities span internal_data (analysts, modelling layer) and finance/commercial (lending decisions, investor reporting) with roughly equal weight.
evidence.autonomy_level: Championing standards and contributing to the improvement of our analytics engineering culture
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role champions standards and proactively raises improvements (strategic) while also supporting analysts and translating requirements (execution).
evidence.ai_role: Comfort using AI tools effectively to move faster, improve quality, and strengthen day-to-day analytical and engineering workflows
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is explicitly expected to use AI tools (including Claude, named in the stack) to accelerate their own work.
evidence.testing_framing: Helping triage and resolve issues that affect the analytics pipeline or reduce trust in downstream datasets
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality and pipeline reliability are discussed but framed as troubleshooting/contributing rather than an owned testing practice with explicit ownership verbs, so tool_listed.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Helping triage and resolve issues that affect the analytics pipeline or reduce trust in downstream datasets
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High: fintech lending context combined with 'reduce trust in downstream datasets' and investor reporting reflects fear of bad data reaching decision-makers/stakeholders.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Helping triage and resolve issues that affect the analytics pipeline
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Dominant verbs are improving, triaging, and resolving issues within an existing pipeline rather than building new infrastructure from scratch.
evidence.work_arrangement: Hybrid roles require three days in-office weekly
evidence.loss_aversion: reduce trust in downstream datasets — fintech investor and lending reporting raises stakes on data accuracy.
